ksa_text: Design and deliver data and AI training and adoption programs, including needs assessment, curriculum development, training materials creation, delivery execution, effectiveness measurement, and AI adoption strategy integration.

dcwf.top_element_label: Ability to interpret and incorporate data from multiple tool sources.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of software design tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.

top_anchors.element_label: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.
Ability to interpret and incorporate data from multiple tool sources.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Operations & Program Management is a primary or secondary domain for the BI Director. This task KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (director).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Operations & Program Management), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.481). External reviewers may question whether this specific task KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the BI Director versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: PMI, TOGAF operational frameworks, and CDO survey data (NewVantage Partners) confirm operational program management as a key competency for data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Operations & Program Management appearing in job postings and role definition literature for BI Director.

decision_rationale: Operations & Program Management task KSA is within scope for the BI Director at the director tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
